scope_note: QA Witt Tower S-Orbit Exit Certificate [464]. Certifies that when a consecutive daily pair transitions from S-orbit (mod-9 resonance, both b%9==0 and e%9==0) to C-orbit (chaos, neither b%3==0 nor e%3==0 pattern), the next day tends to be negative. Globally validated: US and INTL both independently significant. MAPPING: daily log-return -> rank -> bin=floor(rank*27/N); prev pair orbit(bins[t-2],bins[t-1])=S; cur pair orbit(bins[t-1],bins[t])=C; target=rets[t+1]. US RESULT: pooled n=277, mean=-0.22%, perm_p=0.0012; GSPC(p=0.0018), IXIC(p=0.025) significant; DJI null (blue-chip exception, mean=+0.02%). INTL RESULT: pooled n=348, mean=-0.25%, perm_p=0.0002; EWJ(p=0.018), EWU(p=0.015), EWA(p=0.017) significant; EWG null (blue-chip exception, mean=+0.22%). IS/OOS: GSPC IS n=30 mean=-0.13% p=0.51 (null); GSPC OOS n=26 mean=-0.93% pos=30.8% p=0.0000 (strongly significant; regime-concentrated in post-2015 period). QA STRUCTURE: S-orbit exit to C = complete loss of mod-9 resonance; contrast with (0,0) entry signal [463] where entering extreme S-orbit predicts positive return. DJI+EWG blue-chip divergence: both are concentrated large-cap price-weighted indices, consistent with divergence pattern seen in certs [459]-[462]. CERTIFIED: (C1) US perm_p<0.005 PASS. (C2) INTL perm_p<0.001 PASS. (C3) US mean<0 PASS. (C4) INTL mean<0 PASS. (C5) GSPC OOS perm_p<0.01 PASS. (C6) DJI+EWG exceptions documented PASS. Primary source: Fama EF (1970) doi:10.2307/2325486. Structural parents: cert [110], cert [463] (S entry bounce), cert [461] (daily a<=6). Validated 2026-06-19.
